description: POST /users accepts whitespace-only names such as {"name": "   ", "email": "x@example.com"} and stores them. The name field passes the !name check because a non-empty string is truthy. Fix it.
expected_behavior: Returns HTTP 400 when name is empty after trimming whitespace. Valid names are still accepted.

success_criteria.description: Fix trims the name before checking emptiness
Fix stores the trimmed name, not the raw input
success_criteria.rubric: Does the fix both validate the trimmed name AND store the trimmed value (not the raw input with surrounding spaces)?
